economic_notes: Band operates through multiple government business enterprises and partnerships including marina, timber harvesting, fishing licences, environmental assessments, shopping centres, real estate, wood trucking, carbon credits, and grizzly bear tours. Contingently liable for $9,391,587 in member home loans and $17,877,604 for Discovery Harbour Shopping Centre mortgage.
notes: Fiscal year is March 31, 2022 not 2024. Three fully consolidated subsidiaries: Discovery Harbour Holdings Ltd., 1323088 B.C. Ltd., and Campbell River Indian Band Development Corporation. Fourteen GBEs/partnerships accounted for under modified equity method. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $5,271,109 + Ottawa Trust $5,292 + CMHC $102,050 + FNHA $71,861 + Province of BC $730,747 + School District 72 $73,922 = $6,254,981 (School District 72 included as government transfer). OSR calculated accordingly. No schedule of remuneration provided. Wages and benefits per schedule of expenses by object is $2,916,817 but consolidated segment total wages shows $2,916,817 for 2022. Amortization in 2021 was $2,555,483 due to large disposal loss. Band paid off Bank of Montreal loans for Band Office and 6-Plex during the year.
